Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a process tree model and asked to find a trace that the model accepts but that is invalid according to the domain knowledge of Order-to-Cash. Lets first understand the model. The root is a sequence of four children: 1. receive_order 2. check_inventory 3. xor_node 4. close_order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The xor_node is an XOR choice between two subtrees: - successful_subtree: a parallel composition (PARALLEL) of three activities: authorize_payment, package_items, ship_order. - cancel_subtree: a sequence of two activities: cancel_order, then close_order.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now note that the root sequence already has a close_order as the last step. However the cancel_subtree also ends with a close_order. This means that if the cancel branch is taken we will have two close_order activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So if we take the cancel branch the trace would be: Receive_Order, Check_Inventory, cancel_order, close_order, close_order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the domain knowledge says that in the insufficient inventory case we cancel the order and then close the order. It does not say we close the order twice. So having two close_order activities is problematic.

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: In the successful branch the successful_subtree is a parallel composition of authorize_payment, package_items, and ship_order. This means that these three activities can occur in any order

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: the domain knowledge says that in the successful case we should authorize payment, package items, and then ship order. The model allows them in any order because it uses a parallel operator.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: So we have two issues: 1. The cancel branch leads to two close_order activities. 2. The successful branch allows the three activities in any order whereas the domain expects them in a specific order. I will choose the cancel branch trace because it is very clear

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Trace: Receive_Order, Check_Inventory, Cancel_Order, Close_Order, Close_Order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In the Order-to-Cash process when inventory is insufficient the order should be cancelled and then closed exactly once. The trace contains two Close_Order activities which is not allowed
